aliases: Beef Mushroom Burger with Black Mayo

english: Beef Mushroom Burger with Black Mayo

food_subgroup: Beef burger with sauce and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product. The Creamy Mushroom Deluxe is a specific variant with grilled mushrooms and black mayo sauce; no exact FoodOn term exists for this product.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FAN (Hamburgers) is the closest defensible category. No specific code exists for mushroom-topped beef burgers with creamy sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for this commercial burger product. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual fast-food menu items at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one whole burger (approximately 200 g), comprising a beef patty, grilled mushrooms, black mayo sauce, and a bun, as typically served at McDonald's Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Hamburger, single patty with condiments and vegetables (SR Legacy 21110); supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for fast-food beef burgers with sauce
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for the McDonald's Singapore Creamy Mushroom Deluxe. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry 21110 (Fast foods, hamburger, single patty, with condiments and vegetables) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable fast-food beef burgers with mushroom and mayo toppings. Serving size estimated at 200 g based on comparable McDonald's burger weights. Values represent a reasonable per-100g estimate for this class of product. Confidence is medium due to absence of an exact match in any primary approved source.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Hamburger bun / mixed fast-food burger analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed journal (Diabetes Care)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Creamy Mushroom Deluxe. GI of 54 is estimated based on the GI of a standard hamburger bun (GI ~61, white wheat bread category) moderated by the protein and fat content of the beef patty and mayo sauce, which are known to lower the overall meal GI. The composite GI of 54 is classified as 'low' GI (<= 55) consistent with published analogous fast-food burger GI values in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 49.0) / 100 ≈ 26.5, using 200 g serving with 24.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~960 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger, as one serving provides approximately 960 mg sodium, representing roughly 40–48% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2400 mg, and regular consumption of high-sodium fast food is associated with elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI ~54) but high glycemic load per serving (~26.5) due to serving size and carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this burger only occasionally, as despite its low GI, the high glycemic load per serving (~26.5) can still cause a meaningful postprandial blood glucose rise, and the saturated fat content may contribute to insulin resistance with frequent intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.2 g per 100 g; ~8.4 g per serving), trans fat (~0.3 g per 100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to its saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium content, all of which are independently associated with increased LDL cholesterol, arterial inflammation, and elevated cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Deluxe (also marketed as Creamy Mushroom Beef Burger) is a fast-food beef burger sold at McDonald's Singapore locations, including Springleaf Tower and Boat Quay outlets, and listed on field survey data. It consists of a thick, grilled 100% beef patty topped with tender grilled mushrooms and a rich black mayo sauce, served in a soft burger bun. The black mayo sauce is a distinctive dark-coloured mayonnaise-based condiment that provides a creamy, umami-forward flavour profile. The grilled mushrooms add an earthy, savoury note and a soft, meaty texture that complements the beef patty. The burger is classified as halal and is positioned as a premium, indulgent fast-food offering. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 247 kcal, 12.5 g protein, 11.8 g fat (of which 4.2 g is saturated), and 24.5 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of approximately 480 mg per 100 g. A standard serving of approximately 200 g delivers around 494 kcal and 960 mg sodium. The low glycemic index (~54) but high glycemic load per serving (~26.5) make it a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume with caution. The saturated fat and trans fat content, combined with high sodium, make it a food of concern for cardiovascular disease and hypertension management. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary staple.
common_user_queries: What is the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe at McDonald's Singapore?
How many calories in a Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger?
Is the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe halal?
Is the Creamy Mushroom Burger good for diabetics?
field survey Creamy Mushroom Deluxe price Singapore

confidence_reason: No exact entry for the McDonald's Singapore Creamy Mushroom Deluxe exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue (fast-food hamburger with condiments and vegetables, entry 21110) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food data for comparable products. GI is an analogous estimate based on published hamburger bun and composite fast-food burger GI values. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry 21110 (Fast foods, hamburger, single patty, with condiments and vegetables) and USDA Branded Food analogues; no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore product. 2. Serving size of 200 g is estimated based on comparable McDonald's burger weights; actual product weight may vary. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 6. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Vegetables' to 'Meat and alternatives', as the primary component is a beef patty. 7. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. The black mayo sauce composition is not publicly disclosed by McDonald's Singapore; allergen and ingredient notes are based on typical mayo-based sauce formulations. 9. Trans fat value of 0.3 g per 100 g is estimated from USDA data for comparable fast-food beef burgers; actual value may differ.
